fault_hypothesis: On line 63, the condition uses `>` (strictly greater than) instead of `>=` (greater than or equal to), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly allows the notification instead of blocking it.

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit, the condition should be true (i.e., the branch should be taken), but with > it is not.
In the else branch (allowed=True), recent count must be strictly less than limit; if equal to limit we should have blocked.
Capture the value of recent__1 length relative to limit immediately after definition to verify boundary condition handling.
When allowed is False, remaining must be 0; this catches cases where the wrong branch is taken at the limit boundary.
